reviews.title: What a useless building society
reviews.body: What a useless building society.I tried 4 times to make a deposit into my regular savers account using the confirmation code numbers supplied by my bank. Every time, kept on repeating to put in the confirmation code. Obviously have very poor software. So I tried to issue a complaint and this also did not go through as the software did not recognise my postcode. Will no longer be putting further money into this account and advise everyone to avoid.

reviews.title: Avoid at all costs
reviews.body: Could not rate a building society or bank lower than DBS. Constant misinformation on their website, mortgage staff don’t  have an up to date picture, change the goal posts constantly, rude and overall unhelpful.

reviews.title: Fantastic experience today
reviews.body: Fantastic experience today.  Took my grandson who is almost 3 into the branch to pay his piggy bank money in.  I am trying to teach him about savings, so it's fantastic that he can still get a passbook account to watch his pennies grow.The staff were wonderful with him.  The low counter for disabled customers was perfect so that he could see what the assistant was doing.  He was allowed to 'help'  her weigh his money and the staff attitude made the experience so positive that we will be going regularly to do this. Banks could learn a lot about personal service from small building societies

reviews.title: Efficient and friendly …
reviews.body: We've just finished our five year mortgage with Darlington and in my experience they've been so easy to deal with it's been a pleasure. I love how I can email or phone ( we could never do that in the 80s it was into a branch with a stiff wearing a suit) and their advice and help has been spot on. Efficient, friendly what more could you ask. Well done Darlington 👏

reviews.title: I wrote a review of Darlington Building…
reviews.body: I wrote a review of Darlington Building Society 2 1/2 years ago which was not very complementary at all and was fully deserved. Gave them another chance at the weekend by opening their online regular savings account. How things have changed.The account was opened instantly with little fuss and access to online banking with them is good, simple and straightforward. Last time they insisted I had to have an interview with someone before I could open an account - thank goodness that's gone.So overall would highly recommend Darlington Building Society. The only issue is the low interest rates on many accounts compared to elsewhere

reviews.title: Terrible slow
reviews.body: Terrible slow, tills and staff  are not ready when open, always have IT issues. Always long queues .

reviews.title: Great service
reviews.body: Just had lovely lady ( forgot her name we were on phone so long) she talked me through setting online account, it took me ages and she was so patient with me,, Northallerton branch is my local, they know I'm poorly and struggle getting in branch, but if they can help they do

reviews.title: Avoid like the plague - Could care less
reviews.body: Took out a mortgage with DBS which was paid off over 30 years ago. Received a certificate acknowledging charge had been removed.Came to sell the property only to find the charge on the property had not been removed. All it needed was an email saying the charge was no longer valid and would be removed. I phoned Darlington BS who did not return the call. I visited the Redcar Branch,who were helpful, and contacted the mortgage department who refused to speak to me and told me to put it in an email and they would deal with it in due cause. So they are jeopodising the sale going through by their incompetence and laziness.

reviews.title: Outstanding Service
reviews.body: The service we received in the Bishop Auckland branch yesterday was outstanding! My son (16) wanted to open a savings account and as he was off work we decided to go in person to do this. Armed with all the necessary documents (passport, birth certificate and proof of address) I didn't think it would be an issue. To my astonishment after completing a morning bank tour of Bishop Auckland we were turned away by every bank and informed "all accounts are opened online now, we live in a digital world". I expressed in each bank that we wished to do this in person and requested an appointment but was told "this is not something we would ever make an appointment for as you just do it on your phone". Several banks were kind enough to offer support with sitting and helping my son do the application himself - this I found ridiculous as it would have been better customer service to simply open the account for him.We had just about given up and started to head home when we discovered the Darlington Building Society had opened a new branch. We walked in and every member of staff was truly wonderful. We experienced outstanding customer service from start to finish. The cashiers we spoke to asked their colleague if he could help and within 15 minutes my son had been talked through the products they offered and opened an account - service at its best!I am sorry we couldn't remember names but please pass on my thanks to this team. Yesterday morning initially left me feeling totally disheartened regarding the state of this world and how everything needs to be done digitally, but the staff in this branch left me feeling that there is hope. Once again, thanks so much!

reviews.title: Don't open an account with Darlington building society
reviews.body: I decided to open a new account and only had the choice of doing this by post or visiting a branchI unfortunately went to a branch where I was told that I would need an appointment,  No mention of this on line Or to fill in a lengthy form. The assistant was Not friendly and put me off this Society for LifeI decided to go to another Society and opened an account without a problem and will give my savings to them as the Darlington building society didn't want it !!!!!Following your reply 20 hours ago, I will not be giving you my full details as I will never contact Darlington building society again Following my experience. You should give excellent service to All of your customers or do more monitoring of your branch staff

reviews.title: Far too bureaucratic
reviews.body: Far too bureaucratic.   Too much relience on snail-mail, so very slow.Darlington online a nightmare to set up/use.  They post you an id.....??!!Interest rates tended to be a bit better than the Nationwide but such a pain to deal with I am not sure if I can bothered for much longer.

reviews.title: Service gone down hill
reviews.body: Not great when a Darlington building society cheque bounces. (Stockton branch) Closed account due to poor interest rate. Had to go back to branch to sort out. Can never get through by phone always need to leave message. To be fair always get back to you but this can be several hours later. This must be a policy as the head office is the same

reviews.title: Rebecca from the mortgage team has…
reviews.body: Rebecca from the mortgage team has helped us out no end during the period or sorting our remortgage out. She’s always on the end of the line when needed and responds to emails quickly.Also a shout out to Dan who remembered us every time we called.Thanks so much for changing our lives!

reviews.title: Had some dealings with this building…
reviews.body: Had some dealings with this building society recently. Not very impressed.They have sent me an application form for my son to open an account. However they did not include all relevant documents for me to complete, I rang up to ask for them, and they informed me the account is no longer available.This morning they have just sent me all the correct forms. For the account that is no longer available.Incompetent!  it’s like a comedy.
